summary: Raised item translation-unit match coverage by reconstructing several remaining item modules and moving them to Matching in configure.py: itrshell, itkabigon, itmatadogas, itpatapata, and ittools. The PR also made large fuzzy-match progress in itsamusgrapple without marking that TU fully matching, updated item attribute/header layouts, and corrected many symbol entries from global it_* labels to local @NNN labels where the diff showed local rodata/data/sdata/sdata2 constants. decomp.dev reported +5460 matched-code bytes, +17576 linked-code bytes, and 23 new 100% matches, plus six improved itsamusgrapple fuzzy matches.
what_changed: Changed configure.py entries for src/melee/it/items/itrshell.c, itkabigon.c, itmatadogas.c, itpatapata.c, and ittools.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).

Reconstructed previously stubbed or partially unmatched item functions across itrshell, itpatapata, ittools, itmatadogas, and itkabigon; bot output listed 23 new matches including itRshell_UnkMotion5_Phys, itRshell_UnkMotion5_Coll, it_8028DAE4, it_8028E170, it_802E0734, it_802E11E0, it_802EF548, it_802CB4F0, itKabigon_UnkMotion1_Anim, and multiple item .data/.rodata/.sdata2 sections.

Made substantial but not complete itsamusgrapple progress, replacing placeholders with C for functions such as it_802B7160 and it_802B75FC and improving bot-reported fuzzy matches for it_802B9328, it_802B75FC, it_802B99A0, it_802B7160, it_802B7C18, and .sdata2.

Updated item headers to support matching: itSamusGrappleAttributes changed xC from f32 to s32 and gained xA4/xA8/xAC animation pointer fields; itToolsAttrEntry was renamed to itToolsMotionAttrs and used as itToolsAttributes.motions; itToolsAttributes.xC changed to s32; itPatapataAttributes field types/layout changed around x28-x34; itKabigonAttributes was added.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to change many rodata/data/sdata/sdata2 entries from global it_803*/it_804* names to local @NNN labels, with corrected sizes/scopes for item constants and strings.
Added item state table definitions in matched item C files, such as it_803F7AD8 for Kabigon, it_803F7B58 for Matadogas, it_803F88C0 for Patapata, and it_803F5C48 for Red Shell.
smart_moves: Separated fully matching item TUs from still-fuzzy Samus Grapple work: five item modules were promoted to Matching, while itsamusgrapple received large source reconstruction but was not shown as promoted in configure.py.

Used header layout corrections to unblock matching rather than forcing local casts everywhere: examples include s32-vs-f32 fixes in itSamusGrappleAttributes, itToolsAttributes, and itPatapataAttributes.
Kept symbol metadata aligned with the compiler/linker reality by demoting many anonymous constants and strings to local @NNN symbols instead of preserving misleading global it_* names.

Introduced small static inline helpers in item logic, including red shell velocity/stop/acceleration helpers and Kabigon animation helper, which is a common matching tactic when source needs to reproduce repeated instruction patterns.
For itrshell.c, used #pragma inline_depth(8) near it_8028DAE4, indicating deliberate inline-depth control to get the generated code shape to match.
decomp_lessons: For item TUs, reaching TU-level Matching often requires synchronizing C source, item state tables, attribute structs, and config/symbol metadata together; source-only changes may not be enough.
When a decomp type field is really an integer in the original data, leaving it as f32 can block matching or force worse codegen. This PR corrected several such attribute fields to s32.
Anonymous constants should stay anonymous/local in symbols.txt when evidence shows local scope; over-globalizing constants creates misleading names and can fight section matching.
Header comments describing unknown fields must be evidence-backed. Review explicitly called out AI-guessed comments on itTools attribute fields as non-authoritative.
Large generated-looking decomp changes need review scrutiny for semantic comments and names; field names like x0/x4 are safer than confident but unverified descriptions.
naming_conventions: Maintained address-based unknown function names such as it_802B7160, it_802B75FC, it_802E0734, it_802E11E0, it_802EF548, and it_802CB4F0 where no stronger name evidence was provided.

Used item-prefixed motion callback names already present in the project style, such as itRshell_UnkMotion5_Phys, itRshell_UnkMotion5_Coll, itKabigon_UnkMotion1_Anim, itMatadogas_UnkMotion1_Anim, and itTools_UnkMotion4_Anim.

Renamed itToolsAttrEntry to itToolsMotionAttrs and the containing array from entries to motions, but the semantic comments originally proposed for each field were challenged and removed in the final diff excerpt.
Kept unknown struct fields in x-offset style, such as x0, x4, xC, xA4, and xDD4_itemVar fields, when exact semantics were not proven.
Symbols for anonymous local constants followed @NNN label style in config/GALE01/symbols.txt rather than address-derived global it_803*/it_804* names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promoted only item TUs that reached 100% matching according to configure.py changes and decomp.dev results.
Used static inline helper functions to reproduce compiler inlining and reduce duplicated codegen-sensitive logic in item motion callbacks.
Used #pragma inline_depth(8) in itrshell.c around reconstructed shell logic.
Added PAD_STACK calls/padding in reconstructed Samus Grapple code where stack layout was codegen-sensitive.

Represented fixed-point or packed hitbox command data in itsamusgrapple via structured fields and explicit /256.0f conversions, matching observed hitbox setup behavior without naming beyond the evidence.
Corrected local/global symbol scope and constant sizes in symbols.txt to match section data and avoid unwanted global symbol references.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned whether detailed comments on itToolsMotionAttrs/itToolsAttributes fields were human-derived or AI-generated.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.feedback: Acknowledged the comments were AI guesswork and should not be treated as authoritative.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.feedback: Approved after the comment issue was addressed, according to review records.

review_feedback.author: Ari4ka
review_feedback.feedback: Approved after merge-time commit, according to review records.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for completion of src/melee/it/items/itsamusgrapple.c after the 90-98% fuzzy improvements in PR 2430.
Search for final authoritative naming of itToolsMotionAttrs fields before adding semantic comments to itCharItems.h.
Audit remaining NonMatching item TUs near configure.py item list after itrshell, itkabigon, itmatadogas, itpatapata, and ittools were promoted.
Look for follow-up symbol cleanup around it_803B8708, it_803F7B88, it_803F9300, it_804DCA50, and it_804DD948 local @NNN labels.
